# grimoire-wizard

> Config-driven CLI wizard framework for Node.js. Write a YAML or JSON file — get an interactive terminal wizard.

## What it is

grimoire-wizard lets you define interactive CLI wizards as YAML or JSON config files. No code required for simple wizards. Full TypeScript API when you need to go deeper. Works as a standalone CLI, a library, or a non-interactive CI/CD step.

## Install

npm install -g grimoire-wizard

## Key capabilities

- 11 step types: text, select, multiselect, confirm, password, number, search, editor, path, toggle, note
- Conditional branching (when conditions), route-based navigation, back-navigation
- Post-wizard shell actions with {{step-id}} interpolation — missing step-ids throw and abort
- onComplete TypeScript handler file: `onComplete: ./handlers/setup.ts` (runs before actions)
- 6 theme presets (default, catppuccin, dracula, nord, tokyonight, monokai)
- 15 spinner presets via `theme.spinner` (dots, arc, circle, bouncingBar, star, …) + custom frames
- Emoji banner support via `meta.icon: "🍋"` — renders alongside ASCII art
- Spinner events auto-emitted during pre-flight checks, actions, and onComplete handler
- --mock mode for CI/CD, --dry-run preview (shows steps + actions + onComplete), --json structured output
- Answer caching, progress persistence, review screen, wizard pipelines
- Programmatic API: runWizard(), defineWizard()

## Programmatic API options (RunWizardOptions)

- `optionsProvider(stepId, answers)` — async function to load dynamic options for select/multiselect/search steps (shows spinner while loading)
- `asyncValidate(stepId, value, answers)` — async validation that blocks step progression
- `onBeforeStep(stepId, step, state)` — hook to mutate step config before rendering
- `onAfterStep(stepId, value, state)` — hook to react after a step completes
- `templateAnswers` — pre-fill answers as defaults
- `mockAnswers` — answers map for --mock mode
- `renderer` — custom renderer instance
- `configFilePath` — path used to resolve relative onComplete handler paths

## Config shape

```yaml
meta:
  name: My Wizard
  description: What it does
  icon: "🚀"            # optional emoji shown in banner
  review: true          # show review screen before submitting

theme:
  preset: catppuccin    # default|catppuccin|dracula|nord|tokyonight|monokai
  spinner: dots         # named preset or { frames: [...], interval: 80 }

steps:
  - id: name
    type: text          # text|select|multiselect|confirm|password|number|search|editor|path|toggle|note
    message: Project name?
    when: { field: prev-step, equals: true }   # conditional
    validate: [{ rule: required }, { rule: minLength, value: 3 }]
    default: my-app
    checked: true       # multiselect: pre-checked

checks:               # pre-flight shell commands
  - name: Git installed
    run: git --version
    message: Install git first

onComplete: ./handlers/setup.ts   # TS/JS file, default export called with { answers, config }

actions:              # post-wizard shell commands, run after onComplete
  - name: Create dir
    run: "mkdir {{project-name}}"
    when: { field: create-dir, equals: true }

output:
  format: json          # json|env|yaml
  path: ./.output.json
```

## Full reference

## Package

npm: grimoire-wizard
GitHub: https://github.com/YosefHayim/grimoire
Version: 0.5.2
License: MIT
Node: >= 18 (ESM)
